name: St. Louis Metro
description: A bi-state metropolitan area anchored by the Mississippi River, defined by a distinct divide between the independent city core and the sprawling suburban counties of Missouri and Illinois.

name: City of St. Louis
description: An independent city geographically separate from St. Louis County, known for its dense brick architecture, 79 distinct neighborhoods, and the massive Forest Park.

name: St. Louis County
description: The most populous county in Missouri, consisting of nearly 90 individual municipalities ranging from inner-ring historic suburbs to high-acreage western estates.

name: St. Charles County
description: Located across the Missouri River, this area is characterized by rapid new construction, high-performing school districts, and the historic riverfront of its namesake city.

name: Central West End
description: A dense urban district bordering Forest Park, home to the Cathedral Basilica and a mix of turn-of-the-century mansions and high-rise medical professionals.

name: Soulard
description: The city's oldest neighborhood, dominated by red-brick French Second Empire architecture and the historic Soulard Farmers Market that has operated since 1779.

name: Lafayette Square
description: A historic district centered around the city's oldest public park, famous for its 'Painted Lady' Victorian mansions and strictly enforced historic preservation codes.

name: The Hill
description: A world-renowned Italian-American enclave known for its shotgun-style brick houses, neighborhood delis, and a high concentration of multi-generational families.

name: Tower Grove South
description: A diverse area bordering the Victorian-era Tower Grove Park, featuring a high density of two-family flats and the international dining corridor of South Grand Boulevard.

name: Benton Park
description: Historically tied to the city's brewing industry, this neighborhood features 19th-century brick rowhouses and a high concentration of independent restaurants.

name: St. Louis Hills
description: A South City neighborhood primarily developed in the 1930s and 40s, known for art deco influences, gingerbread-style brick bungalows, and proximity to Francis Park.

name: Dogtown
description: A cluster of neighborhoods including Clayton-Tamm and Franz Park, known for its Irish heritage, worker cottages, and direct access to the zoo and Forest Park.

name: Old North St. Louis
description: A historic area featuring significant 19th-century restoration projects, a pedestrian mall, and the famous Crown Candy Kitchen.

name: Clayton
description: The county seat and a major business hub, defined by high-end high-rise condos, a walkable downtown, and some of the highest property values in the state.

name: DeMun
description: A walkable, leafy pocket on the eastern edge of Clayton near Concordia Seminary, featuring pre-war apartment buildings and small-scale commercial corners.

name: The Moorlands
description: A residential area in Clayton known for its high concentration of historic 1920s-era luxury apartments and stately single-family brick homes.

name: Clayton Gardens
description: A prestigious mid-city neighborhood where original mid-century homes are frequently replaced by massive, custom-built luxury modern estates.

name: Kirkwood
description: Established as the first planned suburb west of the Mississippi, it is centered around an active Amtrak station and a historic downtown shopping district.

name: Downtown Kirkwood
description: A pedestrian-friendly district with a mix of historic storefronts, new luxury apartments, and the long-standing Kirkwood Farmers Market.

name: Webster Groves
description: Known as the 'City of Homes,' this area features a massive variety of architectural styles, a private university, and a small-town aesthetic with no through-traffic.

name: University City
description: A diverse municipality bordering Washington University, featuring a mix of grand historic mansions and smaller post-war residential blocks.

name: The Loop
description: An entertainment and residential corridor along Delmar Boulevard, filled with concert venues, ethnic restaurants, and eclectic retail.

name: Chesterfield
description: A massive western suburb spanning the Missouri River valley, characterized by large-scale shopping developments and hilly residential subdivisions.

name: Green Trails
description: An established Chesterfield neighborhood prized for its extensive network of walking paths and 1970s-era traditional family homes.

name: Wildhorse
description: A luxury subdivision in Chesterfield featuring large custom estates, private community pools, and proximity to the Spirit of St. Louis Airport.

name: Wildwood
description: The westernmost city in the county, emphasizing land conservation with strict limits on commercial density and a focus on equestrian and hiking trails.

name: Babler Meadows
description: A secluded neighborhood bordering Babler State Park, offering larger-than-average lots and a heavy presence of mature timber.

name: Old State Farms
description: A rural-style residential area in Wildwood known for scenic ridge views and varied topography that prevents standard grid development.

name: Ladue
description: One of the state's wealthiest enclaves, characterized by multi-acre estates, private schools, and a small, high-end commercial core along Clayton Road.

name: Creve Coeur
description: A central county city known for its medical and tech corridors, mid-century modern architecture, and the massive Creve Coeur Lake Memorial Park.

name: Bellerive Corridor
description: The residential stretch surrounding the Bellerive Country Club, featuring large ranch-style homes and expansive wooded lots.

name: Town and Country
description: An affluent residential-only community with no industrial zoning, known for its one-acre minimum lot sizes and massive custom-built mansions.

name: Maplewood
description: A compact, inner-ring suburb with a revitalized downtown core focused on artisan makers, breweries, and early 20th-century frame houses.

name: St. Charles
description: A historic river city featuring cobblestone streets on Main Street and a wide range of housing from 1800s cottages to new master-planned communities.

name: New Town
description: A New Urbanist development featuring high-density housing, man-made canals, and a walkable village center designed to foster high social interaction.

name: O'Fallon
description: A major growth hub in St. Charles County, dominated by large corporate campuses, new-build subdivisions, and extensive youth sports complexes.

name: WingHaven
description: A master-planned community in O'Fallon that integrates residential neighborhoods with a golf club and a high-tech business park.

name: Streets of Caledonia
description: A modern, high-demand residential development in O'Fallon featuring resort-style amenities including a clubhouse, pool, and pickleball courts.

name: St. Peters
description: A central St. Charles County suburb known for the Rec-Plex athletic facility and a vast array of mid-range suburban housing options.
